checks.item: lib/openclaw-integration.js exists with correct sessions:spawn syntax
checks.notes: Correctly uses 'openclaw sessions:spawn --runtime agent --model --label --timeout --task' syntax. Spawns via PowerShell with proper working directory and environment variables.

checks.item: scripts/team-cli.ps1 properly spawns and awaits subagents
checks.notes: Invoke-PhaseNode function uses Start-Process with -Wait flag to properly await subagent completion. Calls node to run openclaw-integration.js with spawn command.
All commands implemented: create, run, status, show, cancel. Additional commands: spawn (direct phase), list (alias), help.
Invoke-Run implements sequential phase execution with automatic progression. Validates prerequisites before each phase (plan.md before exec, exec/ files before verify).

checks.item: Working directory context passed to subagents
checks.notes: Working directory explicitly included in agent context: '**Working Directory:** ${taskPath}'. Also set via spawn options cwd: workingDir.

checks.item: Proper error handling and retry logic
checks.notes: runPhaseWithRetry implements configurable maxRetries (default 3) with delay between attempts. Validates expected output files exist after each phase.

issues.description: team-state.psm1 is in team/scripts/ but team-cli.ps1 references it as $PSScriptRoot/team-state.psm1. This works because $PSScriptRoot points to team/scripts, but could be clearer.
issues.recommendation: Consider copying team-state.psm1 to task exec/scripts/ directory or using absolute path from TEAM_BASE.

issues.description: No explicit --working-directory flag passed to openclaw sessions:spawn command, relies on process cwd. Could be more explicit.
issues.recommendation: Consider adding explicit --working-directory flag to sessions:spawn if supported.

summary: Team Mode CLI implementation is functional and production-ready. All core requirements met: correct sessions:spawn syntax, proper subagent awaiting, all 5 CLI commands, phase chaining, working directory context, and retry logic. Minor path organization notes only. Score: 95/100 (excellent).
